steps.n: Added vowel marks to make past tense verb meaning 'he sent'
Changed to show who receives the action rather than who does it
Makes the word refer to multiple feminine entities that are sent
Adds 'by/and' at start and makes the word definite
Adds kasra at end to show it's being acted upon by 'by'

steps.m: By the ones (feminine) who are sent
steps.n: Word is already in its final form with both feminine plural ending and case marker
Changed vowel pattern to create a noun meaning 'what is known/recognized', which came to mean 'custom' or 'common practice'

Added tanwin alif (ًا) to show this describes how the sending/dispatching (المرسلات) happens - in a customary or recognized manner. This is called a 'circumstantial accusative' in grammar terms, but simply means it tells us how something is done.
Added vowels to the root letters to make a noun meaning 'the act of blowing'
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Added feminine plural ending
Added فَ at beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: and the ones who blow forcefully
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite ('the')

steps.m: and the ones who blow forcefully (related to عَصْفًا)
steps.n: Added a mark at the end to connect it with عَصْفًا (blowing) in the next verse
Changed the root letters into a noun that describes the action itself. This word describes how the winds (العاصفات) are blowing.
Added ًا at the end to show this word describes how the action is done - like adding '-ly' in English (e.g., 'forcefully')
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Added feminine plural ending
Added 'and the' at the beginning
Added emphasis with shadda

steps.m: and the female spreaders (with emphasis) (subject of نَشْرًا)
steps.n: Added marks at the end to connect it with the word نَشْرًا (spreading) that comes later
Changed the verb into a noun that names the action itself

steps.m: a spreading (object of النَّاشِرٰتِ - those who spread)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action from النَّاشِرٰتِ (those who spread). It's like saying 'they spread a spreading'.

steps.m: to separate or divide things apart

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra on second letter to make it describe who does the action
Added ات to make it feminine plural

steps.m: female ones who separate/divide (in genitive case)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show genitive case

steps.m: to split or separate things apart

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun showing one instance of separation. This word is what is being separated by 'those who separate' (فَٱلْفٰرِقٰتِ) mentioned before it.

steps.m: to throw, to cast, to deliver

steps.n: Changed the final ي to ٰ to prepare for adding the feminine plural ending
Added ت to show this refers to multiple female beings who do the throwing/delivering. This word is describing angels who deliver the reminder (ذِكْرًا).

steps.m: the specific female ones who throw/deliver
steps.n: Added ال to show these are specific beings (angels) being talked about, and ِ at the end because it's connected to what comes after it

steps.m: and by/so the female ones who throw/deliver (referring to angels delivering ذِكْرًا)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect it to previous verses, showing these angels are part of a sequence of descriptions. They deliver the reminder (ذِكْرًا) mentioned right after.
Changed the verb (to remember/mention) into a noun (remembrance/mention) by adding ِ on first letter and ْ on middle letter

steps.m: a remembrance/mention (object of الْمُلْقِيٰتِ - those who deliver)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is receiving the action of delivering. It's like saying 'delivering a reminder' where 'reminder' is what's being delivered.
Changed the action into a noun by adding damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on middle letter. This makes it mean the thing that makes something acceptable.

steps.m: as an excuse (connected to نُذْرًا - as a warning)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is indefinite and is paired with نُذْرًا (as a warning) in the ayah, giving two possible reasons.
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects عُذْرًا (excuse) and نُذْرًا (warning), showing that the angels bring either excuses or warnings.

steps.m: to warn or alert of danger ahead

steps.n: Added the vowel marks ُ (u) on first letter and ْ (no vowel) on middle letter to change from action (to warn) into thing (warning)
Added ًا at the end to show this is one of two types of excuses being given (either عُذْرًا 'excuses' or نُذْرًا 'warnings')
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning of emphasis. This particle is used to emphasize what comes after it.
Added ما at the end which adds the meaning of 'only/exclusively'. Together with إِنَّ, it emphasizes that what follows (you are promised is occurring) is certain and exclusive.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form by adding تُ at start and changing vowels - now means 'being promised' instead of 'promising'
Added ون at the end to show this is talking to multiple people
Basic past tense form showing something happened
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show something that is happening now (predicate of إِنَّ)
Added emphatic lām to show certainty. This connects to the earlier إِنَّ to emphasize that what is promised will definitely happen.
The word got a double-mark (tanween) at the end to show it's describing something for sure, and the long 'aa' sound is written with a line on top instead of a regular alif
This is a word that means 'when' and introduces a future condition. In this ayah, it introduces 'when the stars are erased'

steps.m: and when/then when (followed by ٱلنُّجُومُ طُمِسَتْ)
steps.n: The فَ prefix shows this is part of a sequence of events happening one after another

steps.m: to appear suddenly, to rise (like stars)
steps.n: The root letters combine to form a noun meaning the thing that appears/rises
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the singular noun for one star
Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural, showing many stars
Added 'al' (the) at the start and a u-mark at the end to show these are the stars being erased
The basic meaning is to completely remove or erase something
Changed the vowels to show this happened TO something rather than something doing it. Like changing 'erase' to 'was erased'.

steps.m: it (referring to stars) was erased (passive subject of إِذَا/when)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show this happened to something feminine (in this case, the stars mentioned in النُّجُومُ)
This is a time word that introduces what will happen when the sky is split open
The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this with what came before

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated
steps.n: The root letters describe something being high or elevated
Connected the root letters س-م-و with vowels to make 'he/it rose up' in the past
Added ء at the end to make it a noun meaning 'sky' or 'heaven' - the thing that is elevated above us
Added ٱل at the beginning to make it specific ('the sky' instead of just any sky) and doubled the س with shadda
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the verb فُرِجَتْ (was split open). The آ is just a spelling variation of ا.

steps.m: to split open, to create a gap
steps.n: The basic meaning is about creating an opening or gap

steps.m: was split open (happened to السَّمَآءُ/the sky)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show this is happening to the sky (passive voice)
Added ت with sukun to match with السَّمَآءُ (sky) which is feminine in Arabic
This is a compound particle made of 'wa' (and) plus 'idha' (when). It's used to introduce a conditional or temporal clause.
The root becomes a noun meaning the thing that is formed naturally
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic singular noun
Changed vowel pattern and added alif to show many mountains

steps.m: the mountains (subject of نُسِفَتْ - were blown away)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it definite and ُ to show it's the subject of the verb نُسِفَتْ (were blown away)
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it passive, showing the action happened to something rather than something doing the action

steps.m: it (feminine) was blown away (referring to الْجِبَالُ/the mountains)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت because الْجِبَالُ (mountains) is feminine in Arabic. The mountains are what was blown away.

steps.m: when (introduces the time when ٱلرُّسُلُ أُقِّتَتْ)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. Here it introduces when the messengers will be scheduled.

steps.m: and when (introduces the time when ٱلرُّسُلُ أُقِّتَتْ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: to send or be sent as a messenger

steps.n: The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Changed from singular to plural by changing the middle vowels and removing the long vowel و

steps.m: the messengers (subject of أُقِّتَتْ - were appointed a time)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of the verb أُقِّتَتْ (were appointed a time)

steps.m: to set or appoint a time

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added shadda to emphasize the action
Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show this refers to a feminine subject (the appointed times)
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to make it into a question word
Added kasra to show this connects to the word يَوْمٍ (day) that comes after it
Added لِ at the beginning to show we're asking 'for which' day something was delayed

steps.m: to pass by (like time or a day)

steps.n: Root letters ي-و-م form the simple noun يَوْم meaning 'day'. The vowel marks show it's being used in a question 'which day?'
Added the tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is an indefinite noun (like saying 'a day' instead of 'the day'). It's owned by the word أَىِّ (which) that comes before it.

steps.m: to delay or set a time for something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added shadda to show stronger or more deliberate action
Changed vowels to passive pattern to show the action was done to something/someone

steps.m: it was postponed (referring to يَوْمٍ - the Day)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to match with يَوْمٍ (the Day) which is treated as feminine here, like saying 'it (the Day) was postponed'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'day'. This word is possessed by الفَصْلِ (the judgment), forming 'Day of Judgment'.
Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for/to' and ِ at the end because it's owned by another word (الفَصْلِ)
Changed the verb into a noun. This word is owned by يَوْمِ (day), making the phrase 'Day of Separation/Decision'.
Added 'ال' (the) at start and kasra at end because it's connected to يَوْمِ (day of)
This is an interrogative particle that asks questions. In this ayah, it's asking 'what will make you know?'
Added و at the beginning to connect this question with what came before it
The long alif at the end shows this is a question word that introduces the following phrase 'will make you know'
Basic meaning of knowing or understanding something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to show causing someone else to know
Added alif superscript (ٰ) to show long vowel sound
Added كَ at the end to show the person being made to know is 'you'
This is an interrogative particle that asks about something. In this ayah, it's asking about 'the Day of Judgment' (يَوْمُ ٱلْفَصْلِ) in a way that expresses how great and significant that day is.
Basic concept of a day or time passing
Connected the letters and added fatha to first letter to form basic noun
Added sukun on middle letter to complete the basic noun form
Adding damma to show this is the subject/topic being discussed in the ayah
Basic meaning of separating or dividing things

steps.m: separation (part of يَوْمُ الفَصْلِ - Day of Separation)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make the root into a noun meaning 'separation'. This word combines with يَوْمُ (day) to form the special phrase 'Day of Separation'.

steps.m: the separation (part of يَوْمُ الفَصْلِ - Day of Separation)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ (the) at the beginning to make it specific. The ٱ with hamza is used instead of ا because it comes at the start of the word.

steps.m: of the separation (part of يَوْمُ الفَصْلِ - Day of Separation)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show this word belongs to يَوْمُ (day), making the phrase 'Day of Separation'
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce this warning word. This is a strong word used to warn about something very bad that will happen.

steps.m: woe! (warning to those who deny the truth)
steps.n: Added double-damma at the end to show this word is doing the action of warning. In this ayah, it's warning the people who deny the truth (الْمُكَذِّبِينَ).
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make a proper word

steps.m: on that day (followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - for the deniers)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to show this is happening at a specific time being discussed. This word is followed by 'for the deniers' showing when the woe will occur.

steps.m: to deny something known to be true
steps.n: This is the basic form of lying
Added shadda to show intensive denial
Added مُ at beginning to change from the action (denying) to the person who does that action repeatedly
Added ين to make it plural
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at end to show it's in a special grammar position

steps.m: for the ones who deny repeatedly
steps.n: Adds 'for/to' meaning to the word
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to 'did not' in the past
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'
Added vowels to connect the letters together
Changed from past tense (halaka) to present tense (yuhlik) by changing the vowels and adding a silent letter at start
Added نُ at start to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed final vowel to kasra because it follows أَلَمْ which makes verbs end in kasra.
The doubled و shows emphasis on being first
The doubled و makes the meaning stronger
Added ين at the end to make it refer to multiple people or things that are first
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and a fatha at the end because it's the object of the verb نُهْلِكِ (we destroyed)
This is a basic connecting word that shows one thing happens after another

steps.m: then (connects to نُتْبِعُهُمُ - We will make follow)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and fatha (َ) with shadda (ّ) to show emphasis and connection. This word connects to the verb that follows it (نُتْبِعُهُمُ) showing a sequence - first something happened, then We will make them follow.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show making someone do the action
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start and ُ at end
Added هُمُ at the end to show who is being made to follow. This word connects to ٱلْءَاخِرِينَ (the later ones) which tells us who 'them' refers to.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters
The addition of آ transforms the word into a superlative meaning 'last'
Adds ين at the end to make it mean 'last ones' instead of just 'last'

steps.m: the last ones (object of نُتْبِعُهُمُ - we will make them follow)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite and َ to show it's the object of the verb نُتْبِعُهُمُ (we will make them follow)
This is the comparison particle ك that shows something is similar to something else
Added ذٰ (that) to specify what we're comparing to

steps.m: like that/in that way (referring to how We deal with the criminals mentioned in نَفْعَلُ بِٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ)
steps.n: Added لِكَ to emphasize and complete the demonstrative meaning. This word points back to what was mentioned before, showing that the way described will be applied to the criminals mentioned after.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start and adjusting vowels
Added ending ُ to show this is the main verb. This word connects to بِٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (to the criminals) in the ayah to show who receives the action.

steps.m: to do something wrong or harmful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Adding مُ at the start and changing the vowels turns this into a noun meaning 'someone who does this action'
The ين ending makes it plural (more than one person)

steps.m: the people who do wrong things
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the' in English

steps.m: with the people who do wrong things (object of نَفْعَلُ)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the start shows how the action is done. This word tells us how we do the action نَفْعَلُ (we do)
This is a basic word used to warn of severe punishment. It doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: woe (is the punishment for المُكَذِّبِينَ - the deniers)
steps.n: The ٌ (double dammah) shows this is a complete statement meaning 'woe/destruction will come'. It connects to المُكَذِّبِينَ (the deniers) who will receive this punishment.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun for 'day'

steps.m: on that day (followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - for the deniers)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify this refers to a particular day being discussed. This word connects to what follows it (لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ) to mean 'on that day, woe to the deniers'

steps.m: to deny/reject something known to be true
steps.n: This is the basic form of lying
Added shadda to show intense or repeated denial
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'one who does the action'
Added ين to make it plural (many deniers)
Added لِلْ (li + al) to mean 'for the'
Added shadda on ل for emphasis
This turns a statement into a question
Adds emphasis to show this is definitely true
This adds 'not' to make it a negative question that expects a positive answer. For example, 'Did We not create you?' expects the answer 'Yes, You did create us.'

steps.m: to create or make something new
steps.n: The prefix ن shows that 'we' are doing the action
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show past tense
Added نَ at start to show 'we' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: we create you all (referring to Allah creating humans)
steps.n: Added كُم (kum) at the end to mean 'you all' - showing who is being created. The shadda is just part of connecting the words smoothly.
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it connects 'water' to the verb 'create you'.
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن or م - this is a common rule in Arabic pronunciation to make it flow better
The weak letter و changes to ا and ه changes to hamza to make the word for water
The ٍ at the end shows this is indefinite (like 'a water' or 'some water')
The shadda is added because this word comes after مِن (from), and the alif is written as آ to show it's a long vowel
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite, like saying 'a water' or 'some water'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he was weak) to a strong adjective (very weak) by adding ي and changing middle vowel
Added shadda on first letter for emphasis
Added tanween kasra to show this describes the water mentioned in the ayah (مَّآءٍ)

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added vowels to make it 'he made' in the past tense
Added نَا to show 'we' are doing the action
Added فَ at beginning to show this action follows from something before it
Added هُ (meaning 'it/him') to show who/what was affected by the action
Basic preposition showing location or position

steps.m: in (something that will be mentioned after)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it connects to قَرَارٍ (a settled place) to show where something was placed.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The alif after the second root letter changes this from an action to a place where that action happens. This word follows the preposition في (in).
The tanween kasratain (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and comes after the word في (in)

steps.m: firmly established, secure (describing the place mentioned in قَرَارٍ)
steps.n: Forms an adjective describing something that has the quality of being firmly established. In this ayah, it describes the secure place (قَرَارٍ) mentioned.
Adds the genitive case ending because this word follows قَرَارٍ which it describes
Doubles the first letter to add emphasis to how firmly established it is
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows direction or destination

steps.m: to (pointing to قَدَرٍ مَّعْلُومٍ - a known measure)
steps.n: Added an alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end with a small alif above it. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows movement towards or up to a specific known measure or extent.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning the result of measuring
Added tanwin kasratayn (ٍ) because this word comes after the preposition إِلَىٰ (to). This shows it's indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is being acted upon by the preposition.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he knew' to 'known' by making it a passive form - adding مَ at start, putting a sukun on ل, adding و, and putting damma on م
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on م makes the meaning stronger and more emphatic
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English)

steps.m: to measure, determine, or have power over

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added فَ (meaning 'and so/then') to connect this action to what came before it

steps.m: and so We measured/determined (referring to Allah)
steps.n: Added نَا to show this is Allah speaking about Himself using the royal 'We'. The sukun (ْ) was added before نَا according to Arabic grammar rules.

steps.m: to be good, excellent, or pleasing

steps.n: Changes to a special verb used to praise something

steps.m: and how excellent (is the ability of Allah)!
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this praise to Allah's power mentioned in the previous word (فَقَدَرْنَا - and We determined). The فَ shows this excellence is a result of that power.

steps.m: to have power over, to measure out, to decree

steps.n: Added alif after first root letter and vowels to show someone who does the action
Added ون at the end to make it plural (more than two)

steps.m: the ones who have power (subject of فَقَدَرْنَا)
steps.n:
1. Added ال at beginning to make it specific
2. The alif became ٰ (dagger alif) which is just a writing variation
3. This word relates back to فَقَدَرْنَا (then We decreed) showing who has the power
Added vowel marks to show the basic pronunciation. This word is used to warn of something terrible or destructive that will happen.

steps.m: woe! (as a threat of destruction to المُكَذِّبِينَ - the deniers)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is a complete statement of warning. In this ayah, it's warning the deniers (المُكَذِّبِينَ) of coming destruction.
The middle letter و is weak but stays because it's essential to the meaning
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it readable

steps.m: on that day (referring to the Day of Judgment mentioned in context)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify this refers to a particular day being discussed. In this ayah, it refers to the Day of Judgment that's being described.

steps.m: to deny something known to be true
steps.n: This is the basic form of lying
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Adding shadda (ّ) on middle root letter intensifies the meaning to show repetition/habit
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple deniers

steps.m: for the ones who deny intensely
steps.n: Added preposition ل at beginning to indicate purpose/direction ('for')

steps.m: for the ones who deny intensely (with emphasis)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the ل with shadda
This is a basic negation particle used to negate past actions
Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added نَ at start to show 'we' are doing it, and changed vowels to show it's happening now
Added kasra at end to show this is affected by a word before it
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun for earth
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific ('the earth')
Added fatḥa at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb نَجْعَلِ (we made)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes to noun pattern showing a place or container. This is what is being made/placed by Allah in the ayah.

steps.m: a container/gathering place (object of نَجْعَلِ - 'Did We not make the earth...')
steps.n: Adds an -an ending to show this word receives the action 'we made/placed'
Added vowels to the root letters and doubled the middle letter to make the word mean 'alive'
Changes from 'alive' to 'living ones' by adding alif and hamza at the end
Adds hamza at start to emphasize the meaning of 'making alive'
Adds tanween (double marks) at the end to show these living ones are receiving the action
Added vowels to the root letters م-و-ت to make the word for 'death'
Changes from 'death' to 'dead ones' by adding أ at start and making it plural
The ًا at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: and dead ones (as objects) (connected to أَحْيَآءً)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this word with أَحْيَآءً (living ones) before it

steps.m: to make something into something else, to place, to set up

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'

steps.m: and we made/placed/set up (mountains in فِيهَا)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with what came before. This verb is connected to its object رَوٰسِىَ (mountains) which appears after it.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within' that shows location. Here it connects to رَوٰسِىَ (firm mountains) to show where they were placed.

steps.m: in it/her (referring to الْأَرْضَ 'the earth' mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to 'the earth' (الْأَرْضَ) mentioned earlier in the passage. Since 'earth' is feminine in Arabic, we use the feminine 'it/her'.

steps.m: to be firmly fixed in place

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun describing something that is firmly fixed
Changed from one mountain to many mountains by adding و after first letter - this is a special plural form
Changed spelling to use dagger alif (ٰ) and ى. This word is the object of جَعَلْنَا (We made/placed) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from 'became tall' to 'is tall' to describe the mountains - like changing 'grew' to 'growing'
Added the feminine plural ending because it's describing رَوَاسِي which is feminine plural. The tanwin kasra (تٍ) shows it's indefinite and in the genitive case.

steps.m: to make someone drink, to provide drink to someone
steps.n: Adding أ at the beginning makes this word about causing someone else to drink something. The word مَّآءً (water) shows what was given.

steps.m: we made (someone) drink, we provided drink
steps.n: Adding نا at the end shows 'we' are the ones doing the action
Adding كم at the end shows the action was done to many people ('you all')

steps.m: and we gave you all drink
steps.n: Adding وَ at the beginning connects this action to what came before it
The root letters change to form the basic word for water. The و (waw) changes to ا (alif) and the ه (ha) changes to ء (hamza).
Adding shadda on م and extending the alif adds emphasis to the word

steps.m: water (as the object of أَسْقَيْنٰكُم - We gave you to drink)
steps.n: Adding tanween fatha (ً) shows this word is receiving the action of being given as drink

steps.m: to be sweet and fresh (especially water)

steps.n: The vowel pattern ُ َ makes it mean 'very' sweet/fresh
Added tanween fatHa to show this word is describing the water (مَّآءً) mentioned in the ayah
This is the basic form of the word meaning severe punishment or destruction. It's often used as an exclamation of warning or threat.
The tanween (double damma) shows this is the subject of the statement, meaning this punishment/destruction is what's being talked about.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it readable. The و gets a sukun (ْ) to show it's silent.

steps.m: on that day (followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - for the deniers)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to show we're talking about a specific day mentioned before. This word connects to what comes after it (لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ) to say 'on that day, for the deniers'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he denied) to a noun meaning 'one who denies a lot'
This makes it plural - more than one person who denies
Added ل (lām) prefix to indicate direction/purpose - 'for/to'
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda
Basic vowels added to make past tense 'he' form
Adding اِنْ at the start shows the action is done on your own - like going somewhere by yourself instead of setting something free
The damma (ُ) at the end shows this is a complete present tense verb
Adding وا makes it a command to many people. The ٱ is a different way to write اِ at the start of words.
This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction toward something. In this ayah, it connects to مَا (what) to show what they are being directed toward.
Added an alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the ى to show proper pronunciation
This is a word used to ask questions or connect parts of sentences
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation. In this ayah, it's used to refer to what the people used to deny.
Added damma to show basic past tense form. The weak letter و drops out between vowels.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this in the past (not 'you')

steps.m: you all were (who used to deny)
steps.n: Added م to make it plural 'you all'. This word refers to those who used to deny the truth, as shown by تُكَذِّبُونَ (you deny) later in the ayah.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'by' or 'with'
Added هِ (with kasra) at the end to show this refers to something mentioned before. This word connects to تُكَذِّبُونَ (you deny), showing what is being denied.
Added small ي (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide to show the vowel is extended
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger - instead of just telling a lie, now it means calling something else false
Added تُ at start to show 'you' are doing it, and changed middle vowel to kasra for present tense
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing it together
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding اِنْ at the start shows the action happens on its own, without someone else causing it
The damma (ُ) at the end shows this is a complete present tense verb

steps.m: go forth, all of you! (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed from 'he goes' to 'you all go!' by adding وا at the end to make it a command for a group
This is a basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something. In this ayah, it connects انطَلِقُوا (go forth) with ظِلٍّ (shade), showing where to go.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end, which is a common way to write the long alif sound at the end of words. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added kasra and shadda to make it mean the actual thing (shade) instead of the action (to shade)

steps.m: a shade (possessed by ذِى/owner of in this ayah)
steps.n: Added the tanween kasra to show this shade belongs to something (specifically to ذِى/owner of in this ayah)
Starting with the basic demonstrative particle ذ which points to something
Added kasra to show this word connects to what follows it
Added ى at the end to show possession. In this ayah, it connects to ثَلٰثِ شُعَبٍ (three branches), meaning 'having three branches'
Adding basic vowel marks to make the word readable
Adding alif to make it a number noun
Added kasra at the end to show this number belongs to or describes the branches (شُعَبٍ) that come after it

steps.m: to split into parts or branches

steps.n: Changes from basic root to plural noun showing multiple divisions
Added ٍ (tanween kasra) at the end to show this word describes the shade mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُغْنِى negative, showing that something does not benefit or help
The shadda (doubled L sound) makes the negation stronger, emphasizing that something absolutely does not benefit at all
This root carries the basic meaning of providing shade or cover
Added ي between second and third root letters with fatha and kasra to show something has this quality strongly
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes something in an indefinite way
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُغْنِى (it benefits) into 'it does not benefit'
The و connects this negation to the previous phrase لَّا ظَلِيلٍ (no shade), making a list of negative qualities
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed from past tense 'became rich/free from need' to present tense 'protects/helps' by adding يُ at the start. The meaning shifted to show protection rather than wealth.
This is a preposition that shows something comes from or originates from something else. Here it connects to اللَّهَبِ (the flame) to show what something is from.
The extra َ (fatha) helps connect this word smoothly to the next word
This form makes the action into a thing - the flame itself
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun meaning 'flame'
Added 'ال' (the) to make it specific, and the ending changed because it follows مِنَ (from)
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows

steps.m: indeed it (feminine) (refers to Hell mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added feminine pronoun 'ha' to refer back to Hell (جَهَنَّم) which was mentioned earlier. This connects to the next part of the verse that describes how it throws sparks.
Added basic past tense vowels. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added تَ at beginning to show 'she' is doing the action in the present. Changed vowels to show present tense. This word connects to إِنَّهَا (Indeed she) which comes before it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the noun meaning 'sparks'. This word is what is being thrown (object of تَرْمِى - it throws)
Added بِ at the beginning to show these sparks are what is being used to do the throwing
Added tanwin kasra at the end because it follows the preposition بِ and is indefinite (not referring to specific sparks)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning an enclosed structure. In this ayah, it describes how huge the sparks from the fire are.
Added the definite article 'al' and kasra for genitive case

steps.m: like the palace/fortress (describing the huge sparks)
steps.n: Added كَ (like/as) to show the sparks are being compared to palaces in size
Starting with the basic particle كَ which shows similarity
Adding أَنَّ (with shadda) to كَ creates a stronger meaning of similarity or appearance

steps.m: as if it/he (referring to sparks flying in the next ayah)
steps.n: Adding هُۥ shows what is being compared. This connects to the next ayah which talks about sparks flying like yellow camels.

steps.m: to gather or collect, to be beautiful
steps.n: This root relates to gathering and beauty, which connects to camels as they move in beautiful groups
Root letters got short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the noun meaning camel
Changed the pattern to make it plural (more than one camel)
Added ة at the end to show we're talking about a specific group or collection of camels together
Added tanween (two damma marks) at the end to show these are multiple camels acting as the subject
Added damma and sukun to create plural form. This pattern is used for colors and qualities when describing multiple things.
Added tanween to show this is describing the camels mentioned before (جِمٰلَتٌ). The word means these camels are yellow in color.
This is a basic word expressing severe warning or threat. It's a particle that doesn't come from a root.
The tanween damma (ٌ) shows this word is the subject doing the action in this warning phrase. It's like saying 'Destruction is for the deniers on that Day.'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun

steps.m: on that day (refers to the Day of Judgment mentioned in context)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that specific day'. In this ayah, it refers to the Day of Judgment being discussed.
This is the basic form of lying
Added shadda to show strong or repeated denial
Changes from a verb (he denied) to a noun meaning 'denier'
Makes the word plural for multiple deniers
Added preposition ل at beginning to indicate purpose/direction ('for')
Added definite article ال to specify particular deniers ('the')
The ل at the start gets doubled (shadda) when it comes after a voweled letter
This is the basic attention-getting part that points to something
The alif is written as a small mark above the ه to show it's stretched when saying it

steps.m: This (pointing to something nearby) (describes يَوْمُ - day)
steps.n: Added ذَا which makes it specifically point to something masculine and nearby. In this ayah, it's pointing to يَوْمُ (day).
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'day'
Added ُ at the end to show this word completes the meaning of هٰذَا (this), forming 'This is a day...'
Basic negative particle that negates the verb that follows it (يَنطِقُونَ - they speak), making it mean 'they do not speak'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he speaks (in لَا يَنطِقُونَ - they do not speak)
steps.n: The prefix ي shows it's happening now or in the future

steps.m: they speak (in لَا يَنطِقُونَ - they do not speak)
steps.n: The ون ending shows multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it's negated by لَا (not) to say they will not speak.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُؤْذَنُ (permission is given) into 'permission is not given'
The و at the start means 'and', connecting this negation with what came before. Together with يُؤْذَنُ it means 'and permission is not given to them'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The يُ at the start shows this is happening now (present) and being done to someone (passive)
The ُ and َ vowel marks show this is happening to someone (passive)
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to يُؤْذَنُ (permission is given) to show who the permission is for
Added هُمْ which means 'them' when referring to a group of men or a mixed group. This shows who won't be given permission.
This is the basic meaning of making an excuse

steps.m: he made an excuse for himself
steps.n: Adding اِ and ت shows doing the action to oneself
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at beginning (related to وَلَا يُؤْذَنُ in previous ayah)
Final damma indicates present tense nominative mood
Changes from singular 'he makes an excuse' to plural 'they make excuses'
فَ means 'so' or 'then', showing this happens as a result of something else
This is a common word used to warn of severe consequences or destruction. Added vowel marks to show how it's pronounced.

steps.m: total destruction (warning to المُكَذِّبِينَ - the deniers)
steps.n: The tanween (double damma) at the end makes the warning stronger and more complete.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it readable. The و gets a sukun (ْ) to show it's silent.
Added ئِذٍ to show we're talking about a specific day mentioned before. This word is followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (for the deniers), showing what happens on that day.

steps.m: deny/reject something known to be true
steps.n: This is the basic form of lying
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger to show someone who keeps denying
Adding مُ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ shows who does the action
Adding ين makes it refer to many people who deny
Combines لِ (for/to) with ال (the) at the start
The fatha at the end shows this is in a special grammar position
This is one of the basic words in Arabic that doesn't come from a root. It starts with ها which gets attention.
Adding ذا makes it point to something specific nearby. The alif at the end makes it masculine. In this ayah, it's pointing to يَوْمُ (the Day) that comes right after it.
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukoon on middle letter to make the basic noun. This word is possessed by الفصل (the decision/judgment), making it mean 'Day of Decision'.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. This word begins the phrase 'Day of Decision' which is being described.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks. This word belongs to يَوْمُ (day), making the phrase 'Day of Separation'.
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific

steps.m: to gather or collect things together

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and make it past tense
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action

steps.m: we gathered you all (on the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show the action was done to 'you all'. This refers to Allah gathering all people on the Day of Judgment, as mentioned in the context of this ayah about يَوْمُ ٱلْفَصْلِ (the Day of Decision).
Doubled the middle letter to show this is a quality or characteristic of someone/something
The ين ending changes the word from 'first' to 'first ones' (makes it plural), like adding 's' in English
Added ٱلْ prefix to make the word definite (meaning 'the')

steps.m: and the first ones (being gathered with others)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this group with others being gathered on the Day of Judgment, and added َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if you have a plan' (كَانَ لَكُمْ كَيْدٌ).

steps.m: then if (connects this condition to what came before)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. It's like saying 'so' or 'then' before starting a new thought.
Added basic past tense vowels to show this happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter. This word is describing the state of كَيْدٌ (plot/plan) in the past.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of this preposition

steps.m: for/to you all (related to كَيْدٌ - plot/plan)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to كَيْدٌ (plot/plan) in the ayah, showing who the plot would belong to.
This turns the action into a noun meaning 'a secret plan'. It is the subject of كَانَ (was/is) in this ayah, showing what existed.
This is the basic concept of plotting or making plans against someone
Adding the kasra changes this from the action of plotting to the plot/scheme itself
Shows this word is acting as the subject in the sentence
Adds masculine singular marker to show it's one plot

steps.m: plot against me! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added نِ (nee) at the end to mean 'against me' - turning 'plot!' into 'plot against me!'

steps.m: so plot against me! (command to many people)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a response or result
This is the basic form of the word meaning severe destruction or punishment. It's a noun that doesn't come from a root.
The double-damma (ٌ) shows this word is the subject doing the action in this statement. It's like saying 'Destruction is for the deniers on that Day.'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun

steps.m: on that day (followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - for the deniers)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that particular day' being discussed. This word connects to what follows in the ayah - it's saying 'on that day' there will be something for the deniers.
This is the basic form of lying
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing intensive denial
Adding مُ at beginning and changing vowels creates a noun meaning 'one who does the action'
Adding ين makes the word plural (more than two)
Adding لِلْ (لِ + ال) means 'for the'. The first ل gets a shadda because it combines with the second ل. This word connects to وَيْلٌ (woe) to show who the woe is for.

Adding a shadda (doubled letter) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that المُتَّقِينَ (the God-conscious people) are definitely in gardens and springs.

steps.m: to protect oneself or be mindful
steps.n: Basic meaning is about protection or being mindful
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changes verb (protected) into noun meaning 'one who protects'
Added ين to show this refers to many people

steps.m: the people who protect themselves/are mindful
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to point to specific God-conscious people
Added a small line (fatHa) at the end to show this word is being acted upon by inna (indeed)
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that the righteous (ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ) are located in (فِى) shades (ظِلٰلٍ).
Added ى at the end which is standard for this preposition when written in the Quran. This is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to shade or cast a shadow
steps.n: Root relates to shade or shadow
Root letters compressed into a simple noun meaning 'shadow' by adding kasra and doubling last letter
Changes from singular 'shadow' to plural form for small quantities
Added two dots under the last letter (ٍ) to show this word means 'in/among shadows' when used with في
Root relates to eyes and springs of water
Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean one eye/spring
Changed from single spring to many springs by adding و and changing the vowels to ُ
Added tanween to show it's indefinite (like 'a' or 'some' in English)
Added و with fatha at start to mean 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Changed from 'enjoyed' to 'something enjoyable' by adding alif and kasra, then ة makes it mean 'a fruit'
Changed to broken plural form by adding و after ف and changing middle letters to make فَوَاكِه (fruits)

steps.m: and fruits (from what مِمَّا they desire يَشْتَهُونَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and final fatha for grammatical connection to following phrase 'from what they desire'
Starting with the basic preposition that means 'from'
Added ما which means 'what' to specify what we're talking about
The ن combines with م and gets a shadda (doubled). This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning. This word connects to فَوٰكِهَ (fruits) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Form VIII verb pattern showing the action is done for oneself
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing ending to ي
Adds plural masculine ending to indicate multiple subjects
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he ate' to 'eat!' by dropping first letter and changing middle vowel to 'u' sound - this makes it a command

steps.m: eat! (command to all of you) (followed by وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ - and drink)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to make it a command for multiple people. This command is paired with 'and drink' (وَٱشْرَبُوا۟) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes 'he drank' to 'drink!' by adding alif at start and changing vowels - makes it a command
Adds plural masculine marker to make command apply to multiple male addressees
This command comes after كُلُوا۟ (eat), connecting the two commands with 'and'
Added vowels and ي to make it describe a quality. This word specifically describes food and drink that is both enjoyable and good for you.

steps.m: in a pleasant and beneficial way (describing كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ - eat and drink)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes how to eat and drink. In this ayah, Allah is telling people to eat and drink in a way that is both enjoyable and good for them.
This is the first particle that shows how or why something happens
Added kasra to help with pronunciation when connecting to next word

steps.m: with/because of what (connects to تَعْمَلُونَ - you all do)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to specify what we're talking about. In this ayah, it connects to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you all do) to mean 'because of what you used to do'

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The middle letter و is removed and vowels change to make the command 'Be!'
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he was' to 'I was' (not 'you was')
Changed تُ (meaning I) to تُم (meaning you all) to make it plural 'you all' instead of 'I'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Transforms past tense verb into present tense addressing singular 'you'

steps.m: you (plural) do/are doing (referring to what you used to do in the past)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this is addressing multiple people. This verb describes what the people being addressed used to do in the past, as shown by كُنتُمْ (you were) in the ayah.
This is a special word called a particle that adds emphasis to a sentence
The 'ا' at the end means 'we'
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
The alif with bar (ٰ) and kasra (ِ) change 'this' to 'that', showing something farther away

steps.m: like that (connects to نَجْزِى - we reward)
steps.n: Adding كَ at both ends makes it mean 'like that' or 'in that way'. This word connects to نَجْزِى (we reward) to show 'that is how we reward'
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: We reward (the doers of good المُحْسِنِينَ)
steps.n: Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed middle vowel to kasra (ِ) to show it's happening in the present. The ى at the end shows it's happening to someone else.

steps.m: to do good, to be beautiful

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha-damma-fatha) to make it mean 'he became good/beautiful'
Added مُ at the start to make it mean 'one who' and changed the middle vowels to show someone who does good deeds
Added ين to make it plural, referring to multiple people who do good

steps.m: the ones who do good (object of نَجْزِى/we reward)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end because it's the object of the verb نَجْزِى (we reward) in this ayah
This is the basic form of the word meaning severe punishment or destruction. It's commonly used as an exclamation of warning or threat.
The tanween (double damma) shows this is the subject of the sentence. In this ayah, it's saying '[there will be] severe punishment on that day for the deniers.'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it readable. The و gets a sukun (ْ) to show it's silent.
Added ئِذٍ to show we're talking about a specific day mentioned before. This word is followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (for the deniers), showing what happens on that day.

steps.m: to deny/reject something known to be true
steps.n: This is the basic form of lying
Doubling the middle letter (shown by شدة) makes the denial/rejection more intense and forceful
Adding مُ at beginning and changing vowels transforms to show who does the action
Adding ين makes it plural - more than one denier
Combines 'for' (لِ) with 'the' (الْ) to mean 'for the'
The fatha (َ) at the end completes the plural noun form
Changed to basic command form by removing first letter and adding damma and sukun
Added وا to make the command for multiple people. The small circle (۟) is just a pronunciation mark.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The word changed to a command form (do it!) and the shadda makes it mean 'enjoy a lot/thoroughly'

steps.m: enjoy yourselves thoroughly (command to a group)
steps.n: The وا suffix changes this to a plural imperative verb form

steps.m: and enjoy yourselves thoroughly (command to a group) (connected to كُلُوا۟ - eat)
steps.n: The وَ connects this command with the previous command كُلُوا۟ (eat)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb to an adjective describing the quality of being small/little

steps.m: for a little while (object of تَمَتَّعُوا)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this describes how long to enjoy. It's connected to the command تَمَتَّعُوا (enjoy yourselves) in the verse.
This is a special word that adds strong emphasis to statements, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly'
Added كُم at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing the wrongdoers being warned.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
The مُ prefix makes this describe someone who does the action
Added ون to make it plural. This word is describing 'you all' in the phrase 'indeed you all' (إِنَّكُم)
This is the basic form of the word meaning severe punishment or destruction. It's often used as an exclamation of warning or threat.
Added the damma tanween to show this is the subject of the sentence. In this ayah, it's stating that severe punishment will be for the deniers on that day.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun for 'day'

steps.m: on that day (connected to وَيْلٌ - woe)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that day' being referred to. This word connects to وَيْلٌ (woe) in the ayah, showing when the 'woe' will occur.

steps.m: to deny/reject something known to be true
steps.n: This is the basic form of lying
Added shadda to show intensive denial
Added مُ at beginning and changed vowels to show the person doing the action
Added ين to make it plural (more than two people)
Added لِلْ (لِ + ال) to mean 'for the'
The ل at the start merged with another ل, shown by the shadda
This is a time word that introduces what happens at a specific time. Here it introduces what happens when they are told to bow.

steps.m: and when (introducing what happens in قِيلَ)
steps.n: The و connects this time condition with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is the basic root meaning
Changed the root letters into past tense 'he said' by adding fathas

steps.m: it was said (when they were told to bow)
steps.n: Changed the vowels to show that something was done to someone instead of someone doing it. This is connected to the next words لَهُمُ (to them) in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose - in this case, it introduces who is being told to bow

steps.m: to/for them (those being told to ٱرْكَعُوا۟)
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show this refers to a group of people (masculine plural) who are being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: bow down in prayer! (command to one person)
steps.n: Changed to command form by adding alif with damma at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and removing final vowel

steps.m: bow down in prayer! (command to a group)
steps.n: Changed alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ) and added وا to make it plural command
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَرْكَعُونَ (they bow down), making the meaning 'they do not bow down'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
This is the basic root meaning
Added fathas to connect the root letters and show past tense
Added يَ prefix and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: they bow down in prayer (those who were told to bow down)
steps.n: Added ون suffix to make the verb plural. This word refers to the people who were commanded to bow down (ٱرْكَعُوا) but refuse to do so.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. This word expresses a severe warning of destruction or punishment.
Added tanween damma to show this is a complete statement by itself, expressing a strong warning.
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it readable. The و gets a sukun (ْ) to show it's silent.

steps.m: on that day (followed by لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ - for the deniers)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to show we're talking about a specific day mentioned before. This word connects to what comes after it: 'for the deniers'.

steps.m: to deny or reject something known to be true
steps.n: This is the basic form of lying
Added shadda to show strong or repeated denial
Changed to show the person who does the denying
Added plural ending to show many deniers
Added لِلْ to show this is for/to these people (connected to وَيْلٌ - woe)

steps.m: for the intensive deniers (connected to وَيْلٌ - woe)
steps.n: The ل gets stronger (doubled) to connect with وَيْلٌ (woe), and a fatḥa is added at the end
Added fatha and combined the letters with shadda to make the question word 'which'
Added the preposition بِ with kasra to mean 'with'

steps.m: so with which (speech after it will they believe)
steps.n: Added فَ with fatha to connect this to the previous thought. This word is asking about حَدِيثٍ (speech) that comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: so with which (speech after it will they believe)
steps.n: Changed the spelling to match the Uthmani script, using ى instead of ي and adding kasra at the end
Root letters connected with short 'a' vowels to make a noun meaning 'event' from verb 'to happen'

steps.m: a narration, speech (that which is told)
steps.n: Changed middle vowel to kasra and added ي to make it into a noun meaning 'that which is narrated'

steps.m: a narration/speech (that they should believe in)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of belief in the phrase 'which speech after it will they believe in?'
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'after'. The middle letter has a sukun to show we pause there.
Added هُ with a small و (ۥ) after it to show this means 'after it'. The 'it' refers back to the حَدِيثٍ (speech/narrative) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added alif at beginning to show making others do the action (in this case, making trust become active belief). Added fathas to connect letters.
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
